mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior month signals easier financial conditions, lowers the equity discount rate, and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if FEDFUNDS declines or stays flat but the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative in most triggered samples, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above 4.5% after a low-rate period, tightening raises the discount rate and suppresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if post-threshold episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears in a 3-year rolling backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor income and recession risk, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if unemployment-shock episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal has no incremental predictive power versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in GDP and retail demand, captured by rising GDPC1 and RSAFS, signals stronger revenue growth for equities and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint upswings in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not precede higher 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the full signal and the interaction adds no lift.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and profit-sensitive cyclicals, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive 3-month changes in INDPRO are not associated with above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply below recent trend, it signals demand softening in interest-sensitive sectors and a weaker growth outlook, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if housing-decline episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE identifies an improving labor market, which raises household income expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if simultaneous PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or if one series alone fully explains the predictive content.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising CPIAUCSL combined with FEDFUNDS below inflation signals policy lag and negative real rates uncertainty, which should eventually hurt S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with CPI inflation above the policy rate do not lead to weaker 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is unstable across rolling 3-year windows.
